title: Fluffy Banana Pancakes
description: Soft, sweet pancakes with mashed banana in the batter. No refined sugar needed.
steps: Whisk the flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
In a second bowl, whisk the egg, milk, melted butter, and mashed banana.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry and stir until just combined. Small lumps are fine.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and pour in a small ladle of batter.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface (about 2 minutes), flip, and cook 1 minute more.
Repeat with the remaining batter. Serve warm with honey or fruit.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a rich, spiced tomato and pepper sauce. One pan, big flavour.
steps: Heat the oil in a wide pan and soften the onion and pepper for 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, and paprika and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the tomatoes, season, and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Make four wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook 5-8 minutes, until the whites are set but the yolks are still soft.
Scatter with parsley and serve straight from the pan with bread.

title: Overnight Oats with Berries
description: Assemble in five minutes tonight, breakfast is ready when you wake up.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (120 ml) milk or plant milk
steps: Combine the oats, milk, yoghurt, honey, and chia seeds in a jar.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight (or at least 4 hours).
In the morning, stir again, top with berries, and eat straight from the jar.

title: Creamy Mushroom Pasta
description: A weeknight classic: garlicky mushrooms in a light cream sauce over pasta.
steps: Cook the pasta in salted water according to the packet. Reserve a cup of pasta water.
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large pan and fry the mushrooms until golden, about 6 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Lower the heat, stir in the cream and parmesan, and season generously.
Toss the drained pasta through the sauce, loosening with pasta water if needed.
Finish with parsley and extra parmesan.

title: Chicken Shawarma Wraps
description: Oven-roasted, spice-marinated chicken wrapped in flatbread with garlic sauce.
ingredients: Garlic sauce, pickles, and salad, to serve
steps: Mix the oil, spices, garlic, and lemon juice into a marinade.
Coat the chicken and marinate at least 30 minutes (overnight is better).
Roast at 220°C for 20-25 minutes until charred at the edges and cooked through.
Rest 5 minutes, then slice thinly.
Warm the flatbreads, pile in the chicken, sauce, pickles, and salad, and roll tightly.

title: Baked Salmon with Lemon and Herbs
description: Foolproof oven salmon that stays moist, with a bright lemon-herb butter.
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C and line a tray with baking paper.
Mix the butter, garlic, herbs, and a squeeze of lemon juice into a paste.
Season the salmon, spread the herb butter over the top, and lay lemon slices on each fillet.
Bake 12-14 minutes, until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
Serve with rice or roasted vegetables.

title: Vegetable Stir-Fry with Noodles
description: Crunchy vegetables and noodles in a quick soy-sesame sauce. Faster than delivery.
steps: Cook the noodles according to the packet, drain, and set aside.
Mix the soy sauce, sesame oil, and honey into a sauce.
Heat the oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry the carrot, pepper, and broccoli for 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir-fry 1 minute more.
Add the noodles and sauce and toss for 2 minutes until everything is coated and hot.
Top with sesame seeds and serve immediately.

title: Lentil Soup
description: A warming, inexpensive classic made from pantry staples.
steps: Heat the oil in a pot and soften the onion and carrot for 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cumin and cook 1 minute.
Add the lentils and stock, bring to a boil, then simmer 20 minutes until the lentils are soft.
Blend half the soup (or all of it, for a smooth texture) and return to the pot.
Season and finish with lemon juice before serving.

title: Roasted Tomato Soup
description: Roasting the tomatoes first gives this soup a deep, sweet flavour.
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C. Spread the tomatoes, onion, and garlic on a tray, drizzle with oil, and season.
Roast 30 minutes until soft and caramelised at the edges.
Squeeze the garlic out of its skins and add everything to a pot with the stock and sugar.
Blend until smooth, then simmer 5 minutes.
Adjust the seasoning and serve with basil and crusty bread.

title: Chocolate Mug Cake
description: A single-serving chocolate cake, microwaved in the mug you eat it from.
steps: In a large mug, stir together the flour, cocoa, sugar, and baking powder.
Add the milk and oil and mix until smooth, scraping the bottom corners.
Stir in the chocolate chips.
Microwave on high for 70-90 seconds, until risen and just set on top.
Let it cool for 1 minute — the centre is lava-hot — then eat with a spoon.

title: Classic Tiramisu
description: The Italian coffee-and-mascarpone classic. Needs four hours to set, worth every minute.
steps: Whisk the egg yolks and sugar over a pan of barely simmering water until pale and thick, about 5 minutes. Cool slightly.
Beat in the mascarpone until smooth.
Whip the cream to soft peaks and fold it gently into the mascarpone mixture.
Dip each ladyfinger briefly in the coffee — one second per side, no longer — and lay a layer in a dish.
Spread half the cream over the biscuits, then repeat with a second layer of each.
Refrigerate at least 4 hours, then dust thickly with cocoa before serving.

title: Hummus with Toasted Pita
description: Silky homemade hummus in fifteen minutes — smoother than anything from a tub.
ingredients: 1 can (400 g) chickpeas, drained, liquid reserved
2 tbsp olive oil, plus extra to serve
steps: Blend the tahini and lemon juice first for 1 minute — this makes the hummus extra creamy.
Add the chickpeas, garlic, olive oil, cumin, and salt, and blend until smooth.
Loosen with the reserved chickpea liquid, a spoonful at a time, to your preferred texture.
Cut the pita into triangles and toast until crisp.
Swirl the hummus onto a plate, drizzle with olive oil, and serve with the pita.

title: Garlic Butter Popcorn
description: Stovetop popcorn tossed in garlic butter. Movie night, upgraded.
steps: Heat the oil with 3 kernels in a large lidded pot over medium-high heat.
When all 3 pop, add the rest of the kernels, cover, and shake occasionally until popping slows.
Meanwhile, melt the butter with the garlic in a small pan over low heat for 2 minutes.
Pour the popcorn into a bowl, drizzle over the garlic butter, and toss with salt and parmesan.

title: Beef Chili
description: A big pot of smoky, slow-simmered chili that tastes even better the next day.
ingredients: Rice, sour cream, and cheese, to serve
steps: Heat the oil in a large pot and soften the onion for 5 minutes.
Add the beef and brown it well, breaking it up as it cooks.
Stir in the garlic and spices and cook 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes, beans, and stock. Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered 40 minutes, stirring now and then.
Season to taste and serve over rice with sour cream and cheese.

title: Apple Crumble
description: Soft cinnamon apples under a golden, buttery oat topping.
steps: Heat the oven to 190°C. Toss the apples with the sugar, cinnamon, and lemon juice in a baking dish.
Rub the flour, oats, butter, brown sugar, and salt together with your fingertips until it looks like coarse crumbs.
Scatter the crumble evenly over the apples.
Bake 30-35 minutes until golden on top and bubbling at the edges.
Rest 10 minutes and serve warm with ice cream or custard.
